critical_invariants: IRETQ stack frame format must be exactly: SS, RSP, RFLAGS, CS, RIP (bottom to top) - hardware defined
IDT layout: each entry is 16 bytes with offset[15:0], selector, IST, type/DPL, offset[31:16], offset[63:32], reserved
APIC EOI (End of Interrupt) must be written to acknowledge every interrupt before IRETQ
Interrupt gate type (0xE) vs trap gate type (0xF) distinction must be preserved
CLI/STI pairing must be maintained - never leave interrupts disabled across function boundaries
NMI handler must never execute HLT or any interruptible instruction
Exception handlers (vectors 0-31) must preserve all registers per ABI
APIC base address (0xFEE00000) is hardware-defined and immutable
known_opportunities: Fast interrupt path: minimize register saves for leaf interrupt handlers
EOI optimization: use directed EOI for edge-triggered interrupts
Reduce interrupt latency by keeping handler code within single cache line (64 bytes)
Use IST (Interrupt Stack Table) for critical exceptions to avoid stack-switch overhead
Batch EOI with x2APIC self-IPI for timer coalescing
SWAPGS optimization: avoid redundant SWAPGS when already in kernel mode
MSI-X vector allocation to distribute interrupts across cores for load balancing
